content: Existing Self-Refine frameworks for LLMs can be significantly enhanced by integrating multi-perspective inconsistency detection from recent LLM self-reflection mechanisms, creating a two-layer self-improvement loop that avoids the need for external supervision or training data entirely. This hybrid framework first generates multiple divergent solution candidates, then uses inconsistency between candidates as intrinsic feedback to iteratively refine the final output through Self-Refine's original iterative loop.
